title: Finite ROC AUC Classifier Ranking

solver_reuse.target: finite ROC/AUC bad AUC QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact finite classifier-ranking replay computes AUC 2/3 from six wins over nine positive-negative pairs, while a malformed row claims 3/4, isolating a compact scalar Farkas conflict.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional ROC/AUC rows only when they add distinct tie-convention, multiclass averaging, threshold-policy, calibration, confidence-interval, or numerical-honesty pressure; keep statistical generalization and floating-point classifier claims in theorem or numerical-honesty lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed finite scored rows, class counts, threshold operating point, ROC staircase, and exact rational AUC as deterministic replay obligations.
Route the false AUC claim through a source-linked QF_LRA artifact and checked Farkas evidence after exact replay computes the conflicting AUC equation.
Expose the pack through probability, statistics, finite classifier, ROC/AUC, exact-vs-floating, and learner-query surfaces.

Keep threshold policy, calibration, confidence intervals, general tie conventions, continuous score-distribution theory, statistical generalization, and floating-point classifier behavior marked theorem or numerical-honesty horizon.
